methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Mbodi AI is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Xavier Chi and Sebastian Peralta. The company has raised $4.4M to date. The comparable universe includes one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private peers in Robotics & Manufacturing, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow sector filter—while seven same-sector peers exist, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints when filtered by stage or valuation tier. A Stage-Progression method was therefore the primary lens applied. Using the $4.4M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage step-ups, this method yields an implied valuation of $11M.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single method, supports a median valuation of $11M with a range of $9M to $13M. The key uncertainty is the absence of tight peer benchmarks at Seed stage in the robotics subsector, which limits conviction in the magnitude of the stage-progression multiplier. Further validation would require either identifying additional Seed-stage robotics comparables or obtaining direct investor sentiment from prior rounds.
